CONTINUED TENTATIVE RULING PURPOSES ONLY
Off Calendar Aug-16-2022 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Clarification Of 05/17/2022 Order, And For An Order Staying Enforcement Of The Arbitration Order
Off Calendar Aug-16-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Kyle McLorg (via CourtCall) for Plaintiff (disconnected prior to hearing at 11:27am).
Melanie Bongham (via Zoom) for Defendant/Moving party

Court addresses tentative ruling. Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT 1100 SACRAMENTO STREET HOMEOWNERS' Motion For Clarification Of 05/17/2022 Order, And For An Order Staying Enforcement Of The Arbitration Order is DENIED. THE MOTION IS PREMATURE. CROSS-DEFENDANT, CROSS-COMPLAINANT AND DEFENDANT, 1100 SACRAMENTO HOA SEEKS CLARIFICATION WHETHER THIS COURT INTENDED TO ORDER THE CROSS-COMPLAINTS BETWEEN THE CO-DEFENDANTS TO BE ARBITRATED. NO ORDER ON FILE AS TO THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AS ON AUGUST 15, 2022.

THE COURT'S MAY 17, 2022 RULING ORDERED PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANTS INTO ARBITRATION STAYING THE REST OF THE LITIGATION AND NOT ANY CROSS-COMPLAINTS INVOLVED IN THIS LITIGATION. THIS MAY 17, 2022 RULING WAS NEVER REDUCED TO A WRITTEN ORDER. ACCORDINGLY, THE COURT OF APPEAL REMANDED THE WRIT BROUGHT BY CROSS-COMPLAINT AND CROSS-DEFENDANT 1100 SACRAMENTO HOA BECAUSE THERE WAS NO ORDER PURSUANT TO THE COURT'S MAY 17, 2022 RULING.

ON MAY 17, 2022 THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING ("PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS AGAINST BOTH DEFENDANTS SHALL PROCEED IN ARBITRATION") AND ORDERED THE PARTIES TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312 REGARDING THE FINAL ORDER. THE COURT'S RECORDS DO NOT INDICATE ANY SUBMISSIONS COMPLIANT WITH CRC 3.1312 (OR ANY SUBMISSIONS OF A PROPOSED ORDER AT ALL, COMPLIANT OR OTHERWISE, FROM MOVING OR OPPOSING PARTIES). THEREFORE, THERE IS STILL NO ORDER ON FILE REGARDING MOTION TO COMPEL, RENDERING THIS REQUEST FOR CLARIFICATION OF ORDER PREMATURE.

THE ORIGINAL NOTICE OF MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION FILED ON FEBRUARY 28, 2022 SOUGHT TO COMPEL ARBITRATION FOR THE "ENTIRETY OF ALL CLAIMS BETWEEN ALL PARTIES IN THIS DISPUTE." ALL CLAIMS BETWEEN THE PARTIES AS OF FEBRUARY 28, 2022 (THE DATE THE MOTION TO COMPEL WAS FILED) INCLUDED PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED ON OCTOBER 22, 20221; PARK LANE LLC'S CROSS-COMPLAINT FILED ON OCTOBER 23, 2021; AND 1100 SACRAMENTO HOA'S CROSS-COMPLAINT FILED ON FEBRUARY 14, 2022.

THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING ISSUED ON MAY 16, 2022, AND ADOPTED AFTER ARGUMENT ON MAY 17, 2022, STATES IN THE PART RELEVANT TO ARBITRATION THAT ONLY "[P]LAINTIFF'S CLAIMS AGAINST BOTH DEFENDANTS SHALL PROCEED IN ARBITRATION." THE MOVING PARTY IN THE PRESENT MOTION FOR CLARIFICATION IS THE HOA. 1100 SACRAMENTO HOA IS A DEFENDANT, A CROSS-DEFENDANT AND A CROSS-COMPLAINANT IN THIS ACTION. THIS PARTY IS NOT AND NEVER HAS BEEN A PLAINTIFF IN THIS ACTION. THE CLARIFICATION SOUGHT IN THIS MOTION ONLY PERTAINS TO THE CROSS-COMPLAINTS, AND NOT TO ANY CLAIMS BY THE PLAINTIFF AGAINST THE HOA AS A DEFENDANT. AS SPECIFIED IN THE MAY 16, 2022 TENTATIVE RULING, ONLY PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANTS ARE TO PROCEED IN ARBITRATION. CROSS-COMPLAINTS ARE SPECIFICALLY EXCLUDED FROM THE RULING (COMPARE THE NOTICE OF MOTION AND THE RULING). THE REST OF THE LITIGATION (I.E. CROSS-COMPLAINTS) IS STAYED PENDING THE OUTCOME OF THE ARBITRATION.

PARK LANE IS ORDERED TO PREPARE AND CIRCULATE A PROPOSED ORDER ON THE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION PER CRC 3.1312(B) NO LATER THAN AUGUST 16, 2022 AND PROVIDE THE ORDER TO THE COURT WITH OBJECTIONS/COMPETING ORDERS, IF ANY, NO LATER THAN AUGUST 26, 2022. 1100 SACRAMENTO MAY RENEW ITS MOTION FOR CLARIFICATION AND STAY IF NECESSARY AND PROPER AFTER A FINAL ORDER IS ENTERED. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)
